summary: Matched a batch of player bonus/plbonus routines and refined the surrounding headers, types, and helper inlines needed for those matches. The decomp.dev report credited +2168 matched bytes: six new 100% matches in main/melee/pl/plbonus and three near-matches around 97-98%. The PR also renamed gm_8016F1B8 to gmDecisionGetType and propagated that name into dbbonus and plbonus logic.
what_changed: Implemented multiple previously stubbed plbonus functions in src/melee/pl/plbonus.c, including pl_80038788, pl_80038824, pl_80038898, pl_8003891C, pl_80038F10, fn_80038FB8, pl_8003906C, pl_80039418, and pl_80039450.
decomp.dev reported three improved but not fully matched functions: pl_8003906C at 98.26%, pl_80039450 at 97.37%, and pl_80038788 at 97.44%.
Renamed gm_8016F1B8 to gmDecisionGetType in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, gm_1601.c/h, dbbonus.c, and new plbonus code.
Added static inline plbonus helpers setKindToOne_inline and setKindToValue_inline, both using Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2 and HSD_ASSERTMSG checks against gmDecisionGetType(kind).
Added pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC to src/melee/pl/inlines.h and reused it in plbonus/plbonuslib paths that gate logic on gm_8016AEDC not being 0 or -2.

Reworked plbonuslib helpers: replaced local gm_8016B168/team condition with pl_CheckIfSameTeam, replaced plCheckTotal with pl_CalculateAverage, included MSL/math_ppc.h, and used ABS for float absolute value accumulation.

Expanded and corrected src/melee/pl/types.h layouts for StaleMoveTable and pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, turning placeholder byte ranges into typed arrays and fields such as x7AC[39], x848[30], x904[215], xC78[6], and many D9C-DD0 fields.

Refined prototypes in plbonus.h, plbonuslib.h, pl_040D.h, plattack.h, gm_1601.h, ft_0892.h, and ft/types.h to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or UNK_T with concrete int, void, float, pointer, and HSD_GObj types.
dbbonus.c now calls gmDecisionGetType when deciding whether a bonus displays as a flag-style name or point-style name with a value.
smart_moves: The gmDecisionGetType rename captures observed semantics from callsites: plbonus asserts distinguish Gm_DecType_Flag versus Gm_DecType_Point, and dbbonus uses the same function to decide whether to print only a bonus name or a name plus count.
Centralizing the gm_8016AEDC validity check in pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC reduced duplicate inline condition code and made plbonuslib callsites more readable without changing the underlying condition.
Replacing a void assertion-only plCheckTotal with pl_CalculateAverage preserved the divide-by-zero assert while creating expression shapes that matched average calculations in several functions.

The plbonus implementation used small static inline setters with exact HSD_ASSERTMSG line numbers and assertion strings, which is a reusable way to preserve debug/assert codegen while avoiding duplicate write logic.
The struct-layout work in pl/types.h enabled natural field writes in the large reset routine pl_8003891C instead of relying on raw padding for many tracked bonus counters and bitfields.
decomp_lessons: For bonus/decision code, distinguish flag bonuses from point bonuses via gmDecisionGetType(kind); callsites that write x904[kind] often need an assert that the type matches the intended operation.

When matching large state-reset routines, investing in typed struct fields and arrays can unlock straightforward C assignments and loops; pl_8003891C depends heavily on the StaleMoveTable and pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t layout.

Shared inline predicates are safer than duplicated local helpers when the same condition appears across modules; here pl_CheckIfSameTeam and pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC replaced local conditions in plbonuslib.
Average calculations in this codebase may match better as an inline that asserts total != 0.0F and returns val / total, rather than as a separate assertion call followed by division.
Do not overinterpret all near-matches as finished: the bot report shows pl_8003906C, pl_80039450, and pl_80038788 improved substantially but were still not 100% in this slice.
naming_conventions: Promote address-only gm symbols when semantics are clear: gm_8016F1B8 became gmDecisionGetType.
Use parameter names like player, slot, kind, idx, and gobj in headers when callsite meaning is known, while retaining address-style function names for still-unknown pl routines.

Inline helpers in this PR follow subsystem prefixes where exposed broadly, such as pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC and pl_CalculateAverage; private matching helpers in plbonus.c used descriptive static inline names such as setKindToOne_inline and setKindToValue_inline.
Headers continue to preserve address comments, e.g. /* 038824 */ void pl_80038824(int, int kind); and /* 16F1B8 */ int gmDecisionGetType(int kind);.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used HSD_ASSERTMSG with specific source line numbers and assertion text in inline setters to reproduce debug/assert paths around bonus kind writes.
Used RETURN_IF in places such as pl_80038898 and plbonuslib early exits to match concise early-return control flow.

Used a volatile int temp_zero plus a casted store into xCBC.x0 in pl_8003891C, with an in-code TODO noting uncertainty; this appears to be a targeted matching workaround rather than a fully understood semantic cleanup.

In pl_80039450_inline, walked a pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t pointer by 4 bytes per iteration while testing gmDecisionGetType(i), indicating a deliberate codegen-shape tactic for clearing x904 flag entries.
Included MSL/math_ppc.h and changed fabs_inline usage to ABS in plbonuslib, suggesting the exact math macro/header choice mattered for matching.
Refined function prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS so callers could compile into closer argument and return-value codegen.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported matched code increasing to 32.54%, +0.06%, +2168 bytes, with six new plbonus matches and three improved unmatched functions.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for full matches of pl_8003906C, pl_80039450, and pl_80038788.
Look for follow-up struct-layout refinements around StaleMoveTable x8C0 padding and pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t D9C-DD0 fields.
Search for further uses or renames of gmDecisionGetType and possible enum names for Gm_DecType_Flag and Gm_DecType_Point.
Investigate whether the TODOs in pl_8003891C and pl_8003E978 were later resolved.
